---
title: "Ian Gault"
subtitle: "Data Scientist • Professional Biologist"
about:
  template: trestles
  image: media/109_IanG_2024.jpg
  image-shape: round
  image-width: 15em
  links:
    - icon: github
      text: GitHub
      href: https://github.com/iangault
    - icon: linkedin
      text: LinkedIn
      href: https://www.linkedin.com/in/iangault
---

## About

Master of Data Science candidate (UBC, graduating Jun 2026) and Registered Professional Biologist with six years of experience designing reproducible data systems for biological and chemical datasets. Graduate research in wet-lab spans mass spectrometry, chemical extraction, cell cultures, and endocrine disruption & high-throughput cytotoxicity assays; subsequent work & training in end-to-end ML pipelines, ETL workflows, and data modelling. Expertise in identifying inefficiencies, building datasets from legacy sources, and translating between multi-disciplinary teams to support defensible, data-driven decisions.

## Education

**Master of Data Science** *(in progress, Jun 2026)*
University of British Columbia, Vancouver, BC

**Certificate — Applied Data Science: Leveraging AI for Decision-making** *(2023)*
Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, MA

**Master of Science — Analytical & Environmental Toxicology** *(2015–2019)*
University of Alberta, Edmonton, AB

**Bachelor of Science — Biological Science & Science Psychology** *(with Distinction, 2015–2019)*
University of Alberta, Edmonton, AB

## Experience

**Environmental Scientist** | WSP | Jul 2022 – Aug 2025
Designed data science pipelines in R for environmental monitoring studies; ETL workflows, legacy data harmonization, and modelling. Lead data scientist, scientific communicator, and project coordinator for 10+ clients over three years.

**Environmental Specialist** | Hatfield Consultants | May 2019 – Jun 2022
Led toxicological risk studies and coordinated large-scale field programs and end-to-end data pipelines — from field sample collection to R-based ETL outputs — for 10+ clients.

**Graduate Research Assistant** | University of Alberta | 2015–2019
Used cell cultures, molecular techniques (RTCA, YES/YAS), and Orbitrap mass spectrometry to perform toxicity testing and chemical characterization of oil sands process-affected water.

## Skills

**Data & Programming:** R, Python, SQL, modular pipelines, ETL workflows

**Machine Learning:** PCA, classification, regression, clustering, neural networks

**Statistics:** Descriptive, inferential, Bayesian, causal; temporospatial analysis

**Wet Lab:** Mass spectrometry, cell cultures, molecular techniques, field sampling

**Tools:** Git, GitHub, Make, Bash, Docker, Quarto, R Markdown, Shiny, dashboarding

## Certifications & Designations

- **Registered Professional Biologist (RPBio)** — College of Applied Biologists, BC (2024–present)
- **Certificate, Applied Data Science: Leveraging AI for Decision-making** — MIT (2023)
